name: Cable Lateral Raise
resistance_profile: Cable keeps tension on the side delt through the bottom (lengthened) range, unlike dumbbells.
rom_notes: Raise to ~shoulder height from a cross-body stretched start.
cues: Cable from the low pulley, across the body
Control the lowering into the stretch
execution_steps: Set the pulley to its lowest position and attach a single handle.
Stand side-on to the stack so the cable crosses in front of your body, and grab the handle with your outside hand.
Keep a slight bend in the elbow and raise your arm out to the side, leading with the elbow, up to shoulder height.
Lower slowly, letting the arm travel back across toward the opposite hip to load the stretch.
good_when: You want constant tension that includes the stretched (bottom) part of the range the side delt.
The side delts are a lagging muscle you want to prioritize.
bad_when: No cable station is free — a dumbbell or machine raise is a fine substitute.
You want the simplest possible setup for a very first workout.
